description_long: This is a vintage-style radio receiver with a boxy, rectangular form factor and a predominantly black exterior. The front panel features a cassette tape deck, a speaker grille, a frequency display, and multiple control knobs and sliders, with the model name 'ZR-04' prominently displayed. The top surface houses a turntable with a tonearm, indicating integrated record player functionality. The rear side includes ventilation slats and various input/output ports, suggesting compatibility with external audio equipment. The sides are plain, with visible screws at the corners, reinforcing the sturdy, utilitarian design. The overall construction appears to be primarily metal and plastic, with some glass or acrylic elements for the display and dials.
description: A black, boxy radio receiver with a front cassette deck, speaker grille, frequency display, and a top-mounted turntable. It features multiple control knobs and sliders, with a robust, vintage design.
description_view_0: Side view showing a plain black panel with visible screws and the edge of the top turntable.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying ventilation slats, input/output ports, and a partially visible turntable on top.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with a plain black panel and the turntable edge visible.
description_view_3: Front view highlighting the cassette deck, speaker grille, frequency display, control knobs, sliders, and the 'ZR-04' model label.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
